}

practice_problems.subject: chemistry
practice_problems.problem_text:
Consider a galvanic cell represented as: $$	ext{Zn(s)} | 	ext{Zn}^{2+}(0.1\,	ext{M}) || 	ext{Cu}^{2+}(1\,	ext{M}) | 	ext{Cu(s)}$$. Given that the standard reduction potentials are $E^\circ_{	ext{Cu}^{2+}/	ext{Cu}} = +0.34\,	ext{V}$ and $E^\circ_{	ext{Zn}^{2+}/	ext{Zn}} = -0.76\,	ext{V}$, calculate the cell potential at 298 K.

1. $1.10\,	ext{V}$
2. $1.05\,	ext{V}$
3. $1.02\,	ext{V}$
4. $0.97\,	ext{V}$
practice_problems.hints: ["Consider the Nernst equation for calculating the cell potential under non-standard conditions.", "Calculate the reaction quotient $Q$ using the given concentrations of Zn and Cu ions.", "Remember to include the number of electrons transferred when using the Nernst equation to adjust the potential."]

practice_problems.subject: chemistry
practice_problems.problem_text: Consider a galvanic cell made of two half-cells, one containing \ce{Zn} in \ce{ZnSO4} solution and the other containing \ce{Cu} in \ce{CuSO4} solution. Standard reduction potentials are given as: $E^\circ_{\ce{Zn^{2+}/Zn}} = -0.76 \, 	ext{V}$ and $E^\circ_{\ce{Cu^{2+}/Cu}} = +0.34 \, 	ext{V}$. Calculate the standard cell potential, $E^\circ_{	ext{cell}}$, for this galvanic cell.
